title: Static Accessibility Heuristics, Semantics, and Human Review · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review.
Audit page language, heading outline evidence, landmark purpose, labels, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers without enforcing cosmetic templates.
Identify native control and relationship evidence while acknowledging accessible names and states can depend on computed browser semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Image Alt Context
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6945 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to map static image, structure, language, semantics, and interaction risks to WCAG with review handoff; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Image Alt Context.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Image Alt Context, keep Image Alt Context connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Image Alt Context preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Every image needs non-empty alt text. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-conformance-nonclaim-64tm2" that uses case 6058 to map static image, structure, language, semantics, and interaction risks to WCAG with review handoff. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
